zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Barker於大約1520年出生在英國瓦維其可爾沃爾維頓時，她的父親John Richard Barker33歲，母親Dame Elizabeth Hill21歲。她於1534年之前與Sir Thomas Leigh在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和8個女兒的父母。她去世於1603年11月16日在英國瓦維其可爾斯同勒享年84歲，埋葬在英國瓦維其可爾斯同勒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice Barker于大约1520年出生在英国瓦維其可爾沃爾維頓时，她的父亲John Richard Barker33岁，母亲Dame Elizabeth Hill21岁。她于1534年之前与Sir Thomas Leigh在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和8个女儿的父母。她去世于1603年11月16日在英国瓦維其可爾斯同勒享年84岁，埋葬在英国瓦維其可爾斯同勒。

nameMeanings.name: Barker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a tanner of leather, from Middle English barkere ‘tanner’, tree bark having been used as the tanning agent.

English: occupational name for a shepherd, from Middle English berker, bercher (Old French berchier, bercher, berkier, berker, Late Latin berbicarius, from berbex ‘ram’, genitive berbicis). With the change of -ar- to -er- in Middle English, this became indistinguishable from the preceding name (see 1 above).
Americanized form of German   Berger   or   Barger  .

variants.name: Berger
meanings: German, Dutch, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived in the mountains or hills (see   Berg  ). The surname of German origin is also found in many other European countries, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Russia, Poland, Czechia, Hungary, and Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic topographic names or surnames. As a Jewish name it is mainly artificial. Compare   Bargar  ,   Barger  ,   Barker  ,   Barrier  , and   Barriger  .
French: occupational name from Old French bergier ‘shepherd’ (from Late Latin berbicarius, from berbex ‘ram’). It is also found in England, as a surname of Huguenot origin. Compare Shepard .
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farms so named with the plural of   Berg   ‘mountain’.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Barker was born about 1520, in Wolverton, Warwickshire, England, her father, John Richard Barker, was 33 and her mother, Dame Elizabeth Hill, was 21. She married Sir Thomas Leigh before 1534, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 8 daughters. She died on 16 November 1603, in Stoneleigh, Warwickshire, England, at the age of 84, and was buried in Stoneleigh, Warwickshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alice Barker est née environ 1520, à Wolverton, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, John Richard Barker, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Dame Elizabeth Hill, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Leigh avant 1534, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 8 filles. Elle est décédée le 16 novembre 1603, à Stoneleigh, Warwickshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 84 ans, et a été enterrée à Stoneleigh, Warwickshire, Angleterre.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alice Barker è nata il circa 1520 nel Wolverton, Warwickshire, England, sua padre, John Richard Barker, aveva 33 anni e sua madre, Dame Elizabeth Hill, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Sir Thomas Leigh prima del 1534, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 8 figlie. Morì il 16 novembre 1603, nel Stoneleigh, Warwickshire, England, all'età di 84 anni, e fu sepolta a Stoneleigh, Warwickshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice Barkerが約1520年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアウオルバートンで生まれた時，父John Richard Barkerは33歳，母Dame Elizabeth Hillは21歳であった。1534年以前にイギリスイギリスでSir Thomas Leighと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。彼女は1603年11月16日にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアストーニレイグで84歳で死去し，イギリスワーウィクシュアイアストーニレイグにて埋葬された。

ko.London St Bride's Church Fleet Street, M: 영국 미들레세스 런던 스트 브라이데'스 c후르치 플릿 스트릿
ko.lifeSummary: Alice Barker(이)가 대략 1520년 영국 워윅실르 울버턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Richard Barker(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Dame Elizabeth Hill(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1534년 이전 영국 잉글랜드에서 Sir Thomas Leigh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1603년 11월 16일 영국 워윅실르 스톤리에서 84세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워윅실르 스톤리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alice Barker nasceu aproximadamente 1520, em Wolverton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Richard Barker, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Dame Elizabeth Hill, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas Leigh antes 1534, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 8 filhas. Ela faleceu em 16 de novembro de 1603, em Stoneleigh, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, com 84 anos, e foi sepultada em Stoneleigh, Warwickshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice Barker родилась около 1520 г. в местности волвиртон, варвиккшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Richard Barker было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Dame Elizabeth Hill, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Leigh перед 1534 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 8 дочерей. Она умерла 16 ноября 1603 г. в местности стоунелиг, варвиккшир, Англия в возрасте 84 лет и была похоронена в местности стоунелиг, варвиккшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alice Barker nació aproximadamente 1520, en Wolverton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Richard Barker, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Dame Elizabeth Hill, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Sir Thomas Leigh antes de 1534, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 8 hijas. Murió el 16 de noviembre de 1603, en Stoneleigh, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 84 años, y fue sepultada en Stoneleigh, Warwickshire, Inglaterra.
